title: MKG-RAG-Bench: Benchmarking Retrieval in Multimodal Knowledge Graph-Augmented Generation
abstract: arXiv:2606.26458v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) over knowledge graphs has emerged as a promising approach for grounding large language models, yet existing benchmarks largely overlook the challenges of retrieval in multimodal knowledge graph RAG (MKG-RAG). In practice, retrieval is a critical bottleneck: multimodal knowledge is heterogeneous, difficult to align across modalities, and often poorly served by retrievers designed for unstructured corpora. To address this gap, we introduce MKG-RAG-Bench, a cross-domain benchmark explicitly designed to evaluate retrieval in MKG-RAG. MKG-RAG-Bench is constructed from two multimodal knowledge graphs spanning general and medical domains, and includes carefully aligned question-answering datasets that support controlled evaluation of both retrieval and downstream generation. The benchmark is built using an LLM-based curation pipeline that filters low-utility knowledge, generates structurally grounded queries

Title: MKG-RAG-Bench: Benchmarking Retrieval in Multimodal Knowledge Graph-Augmented Generation
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26458v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) over knowledge graphs has emerged as a promising approach for grounding large language models, yet existing benchmarks largely overlook the challenges of retrieval in multimodal knowledge graph RAG (MKG-RAG). In practice, retrieval is a critical bottleneck: multimodal knowledge is heterogeneous, difficult to align across modalities, and often poorly served by retrievers designed for unstructured corpora. To address this gap, we introduce MKG-RAG-Bench, a cross-domain benchmark explicitly designed to evaluate retrieval in MKG-RAG. MKG-RAG-Bench is constructed from two multimodal knowledge graphs spanning general and medical domains, and includes carefully aligned question-answering datasets that support controlled evaluation of both retrieval and downstream generation. The benchmark is built using an LLM-based curation pipeline that filters low-utility knowledge, generates structurally grounded queries
translated_title: MKG-RAG-Bench：多模态知识图谱增强生成中的检索基准评测
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26458v1 公告类型：新论文 摘要：基于知识图谱的检索增强生成已成为一种有前景的大语言模型基础化方法，然而现有基准评测在很大程度上忽视了多模态知识图谱检索增强生成（MKG-RAG）中检索环节面临的挑战。在实际应用中，检索是一个关键瓶颈：多模态知识具有异构性，难以跨模态对齐，并且专为非结构化语料库设计的检索器往往难以有效处理这类数据。为填补这一空白，我们提出了MKG-RAG-Bench，一个专门用于评估MKG-RAG中检索性能的跨领域基准。该基准基于两个分别涵盖通用领域和医学领域的多模态知识图谱构建，并包含精心对齐的问答数据集，支持对检索和下游生成环节进行受控评估。该基准利用基于大语言模型的筛选流程构建，能够过滤低效用知识，并生成结构上具有根基性的查询。
tldr: 本文提出了MKG-RAG-Bench，一个专门用于评估多模态知识图谱增强生成中检索性能的跨领域基准，解决了现有基准忽视多模态检索挑战的问题。
en_tdlr: This paper introduces MKG-RAG-Bench, a cross-domain benchmark specifically designed to evaluate retrieval performance in multimodal knowledge graph-augmented generation, addressing the overlooked challenges of multimodal retrieval in existing benchmarks.
